# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI pipeline with multiple stages where build artifacts are passed between jobs. The build job creates artifacts (binaries, configs) and deployment jobs download and execute them. Store artifacts for 30 days for team access.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
- stage1_build
- stage2_deploy
  
variables:
    BUILD_CACHE: "$CI_SERVER_HOME/cache" # Path to the cache directory where files are stored for build artifacts. This is a GitLab CI variable, so it can be used within your .gitlab-ci file or anywhere you want in one of our configuration examples
  
job1_build: 
  stage: stage1_build   
  script: echo "Build start" && rm -rf $BUILD_CACHE/* # Clear the cache and remove old files. Not necessary here because we are not deleting anything, just clearing it for fresh artifacts generation in subsequent job2 (Deployment Job)  
                                        	# If you want to clean build folder after every run of this pipeline or if your requirements have changed significantly  , then uncomment below line   
                                           # /bin/rm -rf $BUILD_CACHE/*    
      	         		                                       	       	   					             	 												     	     						              							  			        ^--This is where artifacts are created and saved in the defined BUILDCACHEDIRECTORY. You can use GitLab’s cache mechanisms to speed up next runs or parallelize this process, you have full control over when (how) these files should be stored for a given environment
  tags: build    # Useful if there are any dependencies between jobs like other job depends on it etc.,   These lines specify that only "build" stage will run the following and hence all others won't. In this case, one of them (`Build start `) runs first before triggering next stages
  artifacts: # Specifies where to store generated files in a project level scope by default ie $CI_PROJECT_DIR or . if you want it on the job’scope then use /path/to/store. The following two lines are about how long these will be cached and what permissions they should have
    - build-artifacts  # Name of generated files to store in gitlab cache directory (relative)   It is also possible for artifact generation at different times or with a specific pattern as per requirement here 'build' stands only. The caching feature can work well on parallel execution, so it saves you the load from multiple jobs running simultaneously
    - configs  # Same applies to generated files of type "config" above   It also follows this syntax and cache duration same like artifact generation in general with an addition that 'build-artifact' is only for job scope caching. If file has different location then specify path as per requirement here ie .cache/
    paths:          # Specifies the directories to store files, these can be under project directory (.), user home etc., default will be current gitlab server root folder if not specified otherwise   Useful for passing build artifacts between jobs. Can handle multiple folders in case of distributed cache or shared storage solution like GlusterFS
    - /tmp/artifacts  # Here you have to provide full path where the files should store ie .cache but this is a example, according your project structure and requirements replace it with actual directory   For instance "/var/lib/gitlab" if used in distributed cache or shared storage. Can handle multiple folders as well
    expire_in: 30d # Defines the cached files will be removed after how long this is set to (days) ie "expired-at = date + '+24h' - $DEPLOYMENTSPECIFICDURATION", replace with your requirement.  
    allow_overwrite: true  # Defines if the files can be overwritten when we change expiration time (can false on production) ie "expired-at = date + '+24h' - $DEPLOYMENTSPECIFICDURATION", replace with your requirement.
  
job2_deploy:  # After the build job finishes and artifacts are generated in cache, it triggers this deployment stage to download those files from Cache directory into .tmp folder of current gitlab server if not specified otherwise or you can provide any specific location ie /var/libgitlab but again according requirement.
   - deploy    # This line defines the dependency job so only after "build" task completes it is triggered and hence this will run next before starting execution here, similar to dependencies in case of sequence flow control like stage1_build depends on build then deploys etc.,  Replace these lines with your requirement
   tags: deploy    # This line defines the dependency job so only after "Build start" task completes it is triggered and hence this will run next before starting execution here, similar to dependencies in case of sequence flow control like stage1_build depends on build then deploys etc. Replace these lines with your requirement
   script:  echo 'Deployments' && /bin/rm -rf $CI_PROJECT_DIR/.tmp # Just a placeholder and will be replaced by actual deployment command ie "./deployment-commands" but again according to requirements replace it as per required. This is where the download, compile etc., commands are put for executing build artifacts which have been downloaded from cache before
   allow_failure: false  # In case any step fails after this job runs (either in script or post stage execution), still run next jobs otherwise stops all stages even if they fail. Replace with your requirement but again according to requirements replace it as per required, ie always set true else not using
